verdict_reason: Vacuous-truth PASS pending corpus-completion re-run. (Part A — same-tag doctrine consistency, gate 6.1) The verse carries three tags: shipping-discipline, outcome-detachment, iteration-loop. The runner did NOT inject same_tag_verses for any of these tags into the input payload, which means either (a) BG 2.47 is the earliest authored verse on these tags or (b) the corpus-level join was not yet wired. Per the gate's calibration rule, 'A verse whose same_tag_verses input is empty for all tags ⇒ PASS for Part A by vacuous-truth, with tag_coherence_score: null. The corpus-level re-run at corpus-completion time is the authoritative pass for gate 6.1.' I therefore record vacuous-truth PASS. The verse's stance under each tag is internally coherent: shipping-discipline (the merge proceeds when the four readiness boxes are checked), outcome-detachment (the dashboard reading does not retroactively determine the rightness of the merge), iteration-loop (post-launch dashboard readback is mandatory; outcome-anxiety is not a valid input to next sprint planning). These three stances are mutually compatible and each one is internally consistent. (Part B — cross-reference integrity, gate 6.5) The verse data does NOT declare an explicit cross_references[] array. The counter_example field references 'Chapter 5 and the closing instruction at 18.66' inline in prose, which is not the structured cross_references[] field this gate inspects. Per calibration ('A verse with empty cross_references[] is fine — set cross_reference_integrity_score to null'), I record null. NOTE for the project: the inline reference to BG 5.x and BG 18.66 in counter_example is doctrinally honest — both verses do speak to right-renunciation as the corrective complement to the akarmaṇi-rejection in 2.47 — but it would be tighter for the corpus-coherence run to elevate these into a structured cross_references[] field so gate 6.5 can authoritatively check them. Recommend tracking as a follow-up data hygiene item. No critical issue. PASS.
